info.title: mod.io API
info.description: Welcome to the official mod.io API. You will find everything you need about connecting with mod.io via the endpoints below.

get.description:
Get the current agreement (version) by type. Successful request will return a single [Agreement Version Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/agreement-version-object). __Valid agreement types are:__ __1__ = Terms of Use - [https://mod.io/terms](https://mod.io/terms)
__2__ = Privacy Policy - [https://mod.io/privacy](https://mod.io/privacy)
__3__ = Game Terms - [https://mod.io/gameterms](https://mod.io/gameterms)
__4__ = API Access Terms - [https://mod.io/apiterms](https://mod.io/apiterms)
__5__ = Monetization Terms - [https://mod.io/monetization](https://mod.io/monetization)
__6__ = Acceptable Use Policy - [https://mod.io/aup](https://mod.io/aup)
__7__ = Cookies Policy - [https://mod.io/cookies](https://mod.io/cookies)
__8__ = Refund Policy - [https://mod.io/refund](https://mod.io/refund) There are three ways you can display mod.io agreements. Pick which is easiest and most suitable for your implementation. __1. Linking to__ the applicable agreement using the URLs listed above. __2. Using this endpoint__ to fetch the applicable agreement and create a UI to display it inline. __3. Web browser overlay__ to display the applicable agreement inline. If doing this, we recommend prefixing the agreement URL path with `/legal` to remove all menus, and if wanting to remove all links add `?no_links=true` to the end of the URL. For example use: - https://mod.io/legal/terms?no_links=true
- https://mod.io/legal/privacy?no_links=true
Get the specified agreement version. Successful request will return an [Agreement Version Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/agreement-version-object).

post.description:
Request an access token on behalf of a Steam user. To use this functionality you *must* add your games [encrypted app ticket key](https://partner.steamgames.com/apps/sdkauth) from Steamworks, to the [*Game Admin > Settings*](/platforms/steam/authentication) page of your games profile on mod.io. A Successful request will return an [Access Token Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/access-token-object).

__NOTE__: Steam is the only authentication endpoint that requires their token to be base64 encoded. All other endpoints tokens should be provided as a UTF-8 character string.

__HINT:__ If you want to overlay the mod.io site in-game on Steam, we recommend you add `?portal=steam` to the end of the URL you open which will prompt the user to login with Steam. See [Web Overlay Authentication](/restapi/introduction#web-overlay-authentication) for details.

post.description:
Request an access token on behalf of an OpenID identity provider. To use this method of authentication, you must configure the OpenID config in your [game's authentication admin page](/authentication/openid). A Successful request will return an [Access Token Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/access-token-object).

__NOTE:__ The ability to authenticate players using your identity provider is a premium feature. If you are interested in accessing premium features via your games dashboard, please [contact us](mailto:developers@mod.io?subject=OpenID%20SSO%20Request).

__HINT:__ If you want to overlay the mod.io site in-game, we recommend you add `?portal=studio` to the end of the URL you open which will prompt the user to login with your auth provider if they are on your games page. See [Web Overlay Authentication](/restapi/introduction#web-overlay-authentication) for details.

get.description:
The purpose of this endpoint is to provide the text, links and buttons you can use to get a users agreement and consent prior to authenticating them in-game (your dialog should look similar to the example below). This text will be localized based on the `Accept-Language` header, into one of our [supported languages](/restapi/localization) (note: our full Terms of Use and Privacy Policy are currently in English-only). If you are authenticating using platform SSO, you must call this endpoint with the `X-Modio-Portal` [header set](/restapi/platforms#targeting-a-portal), so the text is localized to match the platforms requirements. A successful response will return a [Terms Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/terms-object). A game that has marketplace / mod monetization enabled should display the refund and monetization terms. __Example Dialog:__ __IMPORTANT:__ It is a requirement of the [Game Terms](https://mod.io/gameterms) with mod.io, and the platforms mod.io is used on, to ensure the user provides consent and has agreed to the latest [Terms of Use](https://mod.io/terms) and [Privacy Policy](https://mod.io/privacy) of mod.io. The users agreement must be collected prior to using a 3rd party authentication flow (including but not limited to Steam, PSN, Nintendo and Xbox Live). You only need to collect the users agreement once, and also each time these policies are updated. To make this easy to manage, all of the 3rd party authentication flows have a `terms_agreed` field which should be set to `false` by default (unless the user has just agreed to the terms in which case it can be set to `true` for just that request). If the user has agreed to the latest policies, their authentication will proceed as normal, however if their agreement is required and `terms_agreed` is set to `false` an error `403 Forbidden (error_ref 11074)` will be returned. When you receive this error, you must collect the users agreement by showing this dialog, before resubmitting the authentication flow with `terms_agreed` set to `true` (for just this request, future requests should default to `false` and mod.io will tell you if you need to recollect the users agreement), which will be recorded. See the full details of the [mod.io authentication process](/terms). __NOTE:__ You must make sure the Terms of Use and Privacy Policy are correctly linked, or displayed inline using the [agreements endpoints](/restapi/docs/get-current-agreement) to get the latest versions. If you wish to display the agreements in a web browser overlay, we recommend using the following URLs: - https://mod.io/legal/terms?no_links=true
- https://mod.io/legal/privacy?no_links=true :::tip The __/legal__ part of the URL is optional and removes all menus and the __?no_links=true__ part of the URL is optional and removes all links. ::: __NOTE:__ You can use your own text and process, but be aware that you are responsible for ensuring that the users agreement is properly collected and reported. Failure to do so correctly is a breach of the [Game Terms](https://mod.io/gameterms) with mod.io. If your game does not authenticate users or only uses the email authentication flow, you do not need to implement this dialog, but you should link to the mod.io Terms of Use and Privacy Policy in your Privacy Policy/EULA.

Get all mod collections for the corresponding game. Successful request will return an array of [Mod Collection Objects](/restapi/docs/schemas/mod-collection-object). We recommended reading the [filtering documentation](/restapi/filtering) to return only the records you want.

x-sortFilters.description: Sort by name in alphabetical order using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `name`. For example `_sort=name` would return games from A-Z.
Sort by the date they went live using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `date_live`. For example `_sort=-date_live` would return the most recently live games.
Sort by the date they were last updated using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `date_updated`. For example `_sort=-date_updated` would return the most recently updated games.

post.description: Create a new collection for a game. Successful request will return a new [Mod Collection Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/mod-collection-object).

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Forbidden

get.description: Get a mod collection. Successful request will return a [Mod Collection Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/mod-collection-object)

post.description: Edit an existing collection for a game. Successful request will return the updated [Mod Collection Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/mod-collection-object).

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Forbidden

delete.description:
Delete a collection for a game. Successful request will return `204 No Content`.

__NOTE:__ You can perform a soft delete or a permanent delete by specifying the `permanent` boolean parameter in the request body.

403.description: Forbidden. The authenticated user does not have permission to delete this collection.

400.description: Bad Request. Invalid parameters provided.

post.description: Follow a collection for a game. Successful request will return the [Mod Collection Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/mod-collection-object).

201.description: Created. The collection was successfully followed.

200.description: OK. The collection was already followed.

403.description: Forbidden. The authenticated user does not have permission to follow this collection.

400.description: Bad Request. Invalid parameters provided.

delete.description: Unfollow a collection for a game. Successful request will return `204 No Content`.

400.description: Bad Request. Invalid parameters provided.

get.description: Get all mods within a collection. Successful request will return a paginated list of [Mod Objects](/restapi/docs/schemas/mod-object).

parameters.name: _offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip. Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: _limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Defaults to 100.

parameters.name: maturity_option
parameters.description: Maturity options set by the mod creator. Combine to find mods with multiple options enabled (see [BITWISE fields](/restapi/filtering#-bitwise-and-bitwise-and))

parameters.description:
Comma-separated values representing the tags you want to filter the results by:

`tags` = Return guides matching all of these tags, for instance tags=one,two,three.
`tags-in` returns guides with any of the tags included in the request. For instance, tags-in=one,two,three returns all guides for the game having any of these tags
`tags-not-in` If you want to ensure guides returned do not contain particular tag(s), you can use the `tags-not-in` filter

Sort by the authors ID using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `submitted_by`. For example `_sort=submitted_by` would return guides from authors who have been registered the longest.

get.description:
Get all dependencies the chosen mod has selected. This is useful if a mod requires other mods be installed for it to run. Successful request will return an array of [Mod Dependencies Objects](/restapi/docs/schemas/mod-dependencies-object).

     __NOTE:__ This endpoint returns all mod dependencies, irrespective of their status, visibility or platform support. If your game automatically installs dependencies, we recommend moderating the use of this feature strictly, to ensure creators are not selecting _soft_ dependencies to promote or credit other mods, and dependencies are checked for suitability prior to install.

     For example: the official mod.io plugins where dependency support is enabled, typically subscribe users to all dependencies, so the mods suitability and synchronization can be managed by the subscription process. Following a similar process is recommend, particularly if enabling `recursive` dependency fetching, given it can result in many mods being returned.

     __DEPRECATION WARNING__: By default, this endpoint does not return dependencies recursively. However, in a future version of the API, dependencies _will_ be shown recursively by default. We suggest that you remove any custom implementation you may have in order to retrieve dependencies recursively, and instead use the `?recursive=true` query parameter to fetch all dependencies in a single request.

parameters.name: recursive
parameters.description: Should the response include child dependencies in a recursive manner? A value of `true` will display dependencies of dependencies up to a maximum depth of 5 where as a value of `false` will only display immediate dependencies.

post.description: Add mod dependencies required by the corresponding mod. A dependency is a mod that should be installed for this mod to run. Successful request will return [Message Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/message-object).

Get all files that are published for the corresponding mod. Successful request will return an array of [Modfile Objects](/restapi/docs/schemas/modfile-object). We recommended reading the [filtering documentation](/restapi/filtering) to return only the records you want.

         __NOTE:__ If the [game](/restapi/docs/get-game) requires mod downloads to be initiated via the API, the `binary_url` returned will contain a verification hash. This hash must be supplied to get the modfile, and will expire after a certain period of time. Saving and reusing the `binary_url` won't work in this situation given it's dynamic nature.

parameters.description:
If the parent game has enabled per-platform files, by default only files which are approved and live for the [target platform](/restapi/platforms#targeting-a-platform) will be returned.

To QA pending files, you can filter results by their current platform status, using `pending_only`, `approved_only`, `live_and_approved` or `live_and_pending`. With the exception of `approved_only` and `live_and_approved` which is available to everyone, all other values are restricted to game administrators.

post.description:
Upload a file for the corresponding mod. Successful request will return the newly created [Modfile Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/modfile-object). Ensure that the release you are uploading is stable and free from any critical issues. Files are scanned upon upload, any users who upload malicious files will have their accounts closed.

         __NOTE:__ This endpoint does *not support* `input_json` even if you base64-encode your file, due to the already-large file sizes of some releases and base64-encoding inflating the filesize.

         __NOTE:__ This endpoint supports files up to a max of 500MB in size. For any file greater than 100MB, we recommend using the [Multipart Uploads](/restapi/docs/create-multipart-upload-session) system.

As an example, this may include properties as to how the item works, or other information you need to display. Metadata can also be stored as searchable [key value pairs](/restapi/docs/metadata), and to individual [mod files](/restapi/docs/get-modfiles).

parameters.name: metadata_kvp
parameters.description: Colon-separated values representing the key-value pairs you want to filter the results by. If you supply more than one key-pair, separate the pairs by a comma. Will only filter by an exact key-pair match.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description:
Comma-separated values representing the tags you want to filter the results by:

`tags` = Return mods matching all of these tags, for instance tags=one,two,three. Only mods which have all tags will be returned.
`tags-in` returns mods with any of these tags included in the request. For instance, tags-in=one,two,three returns all mods for the game having any of these tags
If you want to ensure mods returned do not contain particular tag(s), you can use the `tags-not-in` filter either independently or alongside the other two filters.

__NOTE:__ Only tags that are supported by the parent game can be applied. To determine what tags are eligible, see the tags values within `tag_options` column on the parent [Game Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/game-object).

parameters.name: platform_status
parameters.description:
If the parent game has enabled per-platform files, by default only mods with files which are approved and live for the [target platform](/restapi/platforms#targeting-a-platform) will be returned.

To QA mods with pending files, you can filter results by their current platform status, using `pending_only` or `live_and_pending`.

__NOTE:__ only game admins can filter by this field.

parameters.name: revenue_type
parameters.description: Finds all mods with or without a price. Defaults to free.

parameters.description: Finds all mods that are in/out of stock.

x-sortFilters.description: Sort by name in alphabetical order using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `name`. For example `_sort=name` would return mods from A-Z.
Sort by the date they went live using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `date_live`. For example `_sort=-date_live` would return the most recently live mods.
Sort by the date they were last updated using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `date_updated`. For example `_sort=-date_updated` would return the most recently updated mods.
Sort by the creators ID using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `submitted_by`. For example `_sort=submitted_by` would return mods from creators who have been registered the longest.

Sort by the number of downloads in the last 24hours using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `downloads_today`. For example `_sort=-downloads_today` would return the most popular mods from the last 24hours based on the number of mod downloads.

Sort by the total number of downloads using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `downloads_total`. For example `_sort=-downloads_total` would return the most popular mods based on the number of downloads.

Sort by the total number of subscribers using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `subscribers_total`. For example `_sort=-subscribers_total` would return the most popular mods based on number of subscribers.

Sort by their calculated rating (thumbs up / down received) using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `ratings_weighted_aggregate`. For example `_sort=-ratings_weighted_aggregate` would return the highest rated mods.

post.description:
The purpose of this endpoint is enable users to report a resource (game, mod or user) on mod.io. Successful request will return [Message Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/message-object). __IMPORTANT:__ It is a requirement of the [Game Terms](https://mod.io/gameterms) with mod.io, and the platforms mod.io is used on (including but not limited to Steam, PlayStation, Nintendo and Xbox), to ensure all displayed content is reportable by users. You can enable a resource to be reported (by implementing a dialog similar to the example provided here)[Reports](/restapi/reports) or linking to the report page on mod.io. __NOTE:__ If implementing your own report dialog, the Terms of Use and Privacy Policy must be correctly linked, or displayed inline using the [agreements endpoints](/restapi/docs/get-current-agreement) to get the latest versions. If you wish to display the agreements in a web browser overlay, we recommend using the following URLs: - https://mod.io/legal/terms?no_links=true
- https://mod.io/legal/privacy?no_links=true :::tip The __/legal__ part of the URL is optional and removes all menus and the __?no_links=true__ part of the URL is optional and removes all links. ::: __NOTE:__ If you prefer to display the report page in a web browser overlay, and you know the resource you want to report, the best URL to use is: __https://mod.io/report/`resource`/`id`/widget__ For example to report a mod with an ID of 1 the URL would be: [https://mod.io/report/`mods`/`1`/widget](https://mod.io/report/mods/1/widget). If you don't know the ID of the resource you wish to report, you can use the generic report URL: [https://mod.io/report/widget](https://mod.io/report/widget) __NOTE:__ If you are a game owner or manager, you can [view all reports](https://mod.io/content) submitted for your game(s), and you are responsible for actioning reports. You can also configure in your game's control panel the number of reports required before content is automatically taken down for review. Read our [Terms of Use](https://mod.io/terms/widget) for information about what is/isn't acceptable.

post.description:
Create a service-to-service (S2S) transaction intent. This is for performing an external token transaction. You should generate the intent, consume the item on your server's backend before committing the transaction. A successful request will return a [S2S Pay Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/s-2-s-pay-object) object. Requires scope of monetization on token.
     Further details about how the [Service to Service functionality](/monetization/studio-as-purchase-server) works and can be used to operate your own purchase server is recommended reading prior to implementing this endpoint.

parameters.name: X-Modio-Delegation-Token
parameters.description: The Delegation Token of the user you are making the request on behalf of.

parameters.name: X-Modio-Idempotent-Key
parameters.description: In the event of a connection error, you can confidently reattempt the request without the concern of inadvertently creating a duplicate purchase or executing the purchase twice. All idempotent keys last 24 hours.

post.description:
Create a service-to-service (S2S) transaction commit. This is for performing an external token transaction. This step finalizes the transaction and will issue tokens to the user associated to it. A successful request will return a [S2S Pay Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/s-2-s-pay-object) object. Requires scope of monetization on token.
     Further details about how the [Service to Service functionality](/monetization/studio-as-purchase-server) works and can be used to operate your own purchase server is recommended reading prior to implementing this endpoint.

parameters.name: X-Modio-Idempotent-Key
parameters.description: In the event of a connection error, you can confidently reattempt the request without the concern of inadvertently creating a duplicate purchase or executing the purchase twice. All idempotent keys last 24 hours.

post.description:
Create a service-to-service (S2S) transaction clawback. This is for unwinding a token transaction. You may use the transaction_id or the gateway_uuid from the previous calls. A successful request will return a [Refund Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/refund-object) object. Requires scope of monetization on token.
     Further details about how the [Service to Service functionality](/monetization/studio-as-purchase-server) works and can be used to operate your own purchase server is recommended reading prior to implementing this endpoint.

__Deprecated__: This endpoint is deprecated for in-game use and will be removed at a later date. As of March 31st 2024, events will only be returned for existing games for legacy reasons. Any new game should use the [Get User Subscriptions](/restapi/docs/get-user-subscriptions) endpoint to fetch the latest mods subscribed to by the authenticated user as this endpoint will no longer return events for games created after that date. If you have any concerns please [reach out to us](https://support.mod.io).

Get events that have been fired specific to the user. Successful request will return an array of [User Event Objects](/restapi/docs/schemas/user-event-object). We recommended reading the [filtering documentation](/restapi/filtering) to return only the records you want.

get.description:
Get all modfiles the _authenticated user_ uploaded. Successful request will return an array of [Modfile Objects](/restapi/docs/schemas/modfile-object). We recommended reading the [filtering documentation](/restapi/filtering) to return only the records you want.

     __NOTE:__ If the [game](/restapi/docs/get-game) requires mod downloads to be initiated via the API, the `binary_url` returned will contain a verification hash. This hash must be supplied to get the modfile, and will expire after a certain period of time. Saving and reusing the `binary_url` won't work in this situation given it's dynamic nature.

parameters.description: Unix timestamp of date file was added.

parameters.name: date_scanned
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of date file was virus scanned.

parameters.name: virus_status
parameters.description: Current virus scan status of the file. For newly added files that have yet to be scanned this field will change frequently until a scan is complete

parameters.name: filesize
parameters.description: Size of the file in bytes.

parameters.name: metadata_blob
parameters.description: Metadata that is designed to be handled by the game client. As an example, this may include properties as to how the item works, or other information you need to display. Metadata can also be stored as searchable [key value pairs](/restapi/docs/metadata), and to individual [mod files](/restapi/docs/get-modfiles).

parameters.name: platform_status
parameters.description:
If the parent game has enabled per-platform files, by default only files which are approved and live for the [target platform](/restapi/platforms#targeting-a-platform) will be returned.

To request pending files, you can filter results by their current platform status, using `pending_only`, `approved_only`, `live_and_approved` or `live_and_pending`.

get.description: Get all games the _authenticated user_ added or is a team member of. Successful request will return an array of [Game Objects](/restapi/docs/schemas/game-object). We recommended reading the [filtering documentation](/restapi/filtering) to return only the records you want.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Status of the game (only admins can filter by this field, see [status and visibility](/restapi/status-and-visibility) for details).

parameters.name: submitted_by
parameters.description: Unique id of the user who has ownership of the game.

parameters.name: date_added
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of date game was registered.

parameters.name: date_updated
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of date game was updated.

parameters.name: date_live
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of date game was set live.

parameters.name: name_id
parameters.description: Path for the game on mod.io. For example: https://mod.io/g/gamename

parameters.name: summary
parameters.description: Summary of the games mod support.

parameters.name: instructions_url
parameters.description: Link to a mod.io guide, modding wiki or a page where modders can learn how to make and submit mods.

parameters.name: ugc_name
parameters.description: Word used to describe user-generated content (mods, items, addons etc).

parameters.name: presentation_option
parameters.description: Presentation style used on the mod.io website.

parameters.name: curation_option
parameters.description: Curation options enabled by this game to approve mods

parameters.name: profanity_option
parameters.description: Profanity option enabled for this game, to scan scan mods, collections, guides and comment associated with the game.

parameters.name: dependency_option
parameters.description: Dependency option for this game's mods.

parameters.name: community_options
parameters.description: Community features enabled for this game. Combine to find games with multiple options enabled (see [BITWISE fields](/restapi/filtering#-bitwise-and-bitwise-and))

parameters.name: monetization_options
parameters.description: Monetization options enabled for this game. Combine to find games with multiple options enabled (see [BITWISE fields](/restapi/filtering#-bitwise-and-bitwise-and))

parameters.name: api_access_options
parameters.description: Level of API access allowed by this game. Combine to find games with multiple options enabled (see [BITWISE fields](/restapi/filtering#-bitwise-and-bitwise-and))

parameters.name: maturity_options
parameters.description: Mature content setup for this game

parameters.name: show_hidden_tags
parameters.description: Show the hidden tags associated with the given game.

x-sortFilters.description: Sort by name in alphabetical order using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `name`. For example `_sort=name` would return games from A-Z.
Sort by the date they went live using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `date_live`. For example `_sort=-date_live` would return the most recently live games.
Sort by the date they were last updated using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `date_updated`. For example `_sort=-date_updated` would return the most recently updated games.

Sort by the number of mod downloads in the last 24hours using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `downloads_today`. For example `_sort=-downloads_today` would return the most popular games from the last 24hours based on the number of mod downloads.

Sort by the total number of mod downloads using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `downloads_total`. For example `_sort=-downloads_total` would return the most popular games based on the number of mod downloads.

Sort by the total number of mod subscribers using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `subscribers_total`. For example `_sort=-subscribers_total` would return the most popular games based on number of mod subscribers.
Sort by the number of mods using the [_sort query parameter](/restapi/sorting) `mods_count_total`. For example `_sort=-mods_count_total` would return the games with the most number of mods submitted.

parameters.description: Unix timestamp of date mod was updated.

parameters.name: date_live
parameters.description: Unix timestamp of date mod was set live.

parameters.name: modfile
parameters.description: Unique id of the file that is the current active release.

parameters.name: metadata_kvp
parameters.description: Colon-separated values representing the key-value pairs you want to filter the results by. If you supply more than one key-pair, separate the pairs by a comma. Will only filter by an exact key-pair match.

parameters.name: metadata_blob
parameters.description: Metadata that accepts any string-based arbitrary value, up to a maximum of 50,000 characters.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Comma-separated values representing the tags you want to filter the results by. If you specify multiple tags, only mods which have all tags will be returned, and only tags that are supported by the parent game can be applied. To determine what tags are eligible, see the tags values within `tag_options` column on the parent [Game Object](/restapi/docs/schemas/game-object). If you want to ensure mods returned do not contain particular tag(s), you can use the `tags-not-in` filter either independently or alongside this filter.

parameters.name: platform_status
parameters.description:
If the parent game has enabled per-platform files, by default only mods with files which are approved and live for the [target platform](/restapi/platforms#targeting-a-platform) will be returned.

To QA mods with pending files, you can filter results by their current platform status, using `pending_only` or `live_and_pending`.

__NOTE:__ only game admins can filter by this field.

Note: To target a platform for this endpoint, you MUST also include the `game_id` filter for the game that has cross-platform filtering enabled.

__NOTE:__ Users can unsubscribe from mods via mod.io, we recommend you poll or call the [Get Mod Events](/restapi/docs/get-mod-events) endpoint when needed, to keep a users mods collection up to date.

get.description: Get all monetization transactions for the given monetization team. Successful request will return a `200 - Success` response. Further details about how the [Service to Service functionality](/monetization/studio-as-purchase-server) works and can be used to operate your own purchase server is recommended reading prior to implementing this endpoint.

buyer.description: The buyer id of who made the transaction to filter by. Can only be used as a single filter.

clawback_uuid.description: The unique identifier submitted during the transaction that can be used to trace a clawback event.

gateway_uuid.description: The unique identifier created during the transaction that can be used to trace a transaction.

line_items.description: The line_item key value to filter by. Can only be used as a single filter. Filtering can be done by key value on the line item. i.e. mod_id:1 or buyer_id:1

created_at_start.description: Unix timestamp of date to start filtering transactions from. Assumes the end date to current if not given.

Further details about how the [Service to Service functionality](/monetization/studio-as-purchase-server) works and can be used to operate your own purchase server is recommended reading prior to implementing this endpoint.

name_id.description: Path for the collection on mod.io. For example: https://mod.io/g/gamename/c/collectionname.

tags.description:
Comma-separated values representing the tags you want to filter the results by.

`tags` = Return collections matching all of these tags, for instance tags=one,two,three.
`tags-in` returns collections with any of the tags included in the request. For instance, tags-in=one,two,three returns all collections for the game having any of these tags.
If you want to ensure collections returned do not contain particular tag(s), you can use the `tags-not-in` filter.

permanent.description: Whether to permanently delete the collection. Default is false.

name_id.description: The unique slug for the collection.

summary.description: A brief summary of the collection.
summary.example: This is a collection of the best mods.

category.example:
Available options are:

- * Essential
- * Themed
- * Miscellaneous

description.description: A detailed description of the collection.
description.example: This collection includes mods for enhancing gameplay.

logo.description: The logo image file for the collection.

mod_ids.description: IDs of the mods to add to the collection, to perform a sync, provide the mod IDs you want to keep in the collection as well as any new mods you would like to add. If `sync` is set to true, any mods not included will be removed from the collection. If empty, and `sync` is set to true, all mods will be removed from the collection

sync.description: Whether to sync the collection with the provided mods, any mods not included while sync is `true` will result in those mods being removed from the collection. Default is false.

workflow.description: The workflow of the webhook (either initialize or finalize).

cloud_cooking_uri.description: The URI of the cook server.

logo.description:
Image file which will represent your mods logo. Must be jpg, jpeg or png format and cannot exceed 8MB in filesize. Dimensions must be at least 512x288 and we recommended you supply a high resolution image with a 16 / 9 ratio. mod.io will use this logo to create three thumbnails with the dimensions of 320x180, 640x360 and 1280x720.

Alternatively, a zip archive of images to add to the mods gallery. Only valid jpg, jpeg and png images in the zip file will be processed. The filename __must be images.zip__ all other zips will be ignored. Alternatively you can POST one or more images to this endpoint and they will be detected and added to the mods gallery.

sync.description:
Defaults to false, which will append uploaded images to the gallery. Passing in true will
                    allow you to remove all images that exist in the gallery and be replaced with the uploaded images.

youtube.description:
Full Youtube link(s) you want to add. Every Youtube link to add requires a separate field
                    with youtube[] as the key (eg. youtube[]=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IGVZOLV9SPo,
                    youtube[]=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5nY6fjZ3EUc)

sketchfab.description:
Full Sketchfab link(s) you want to add. Every Sketchfab link to add requires a separate field
                    with sketchfab[] as the key (eg. sketchfab[]=https://sketchfab.com/models/71f04e390ff54e5f8d9a51b4e1caab7e,
                    sketchfab[]=https://sketchfab.com/models/5c85e649dd854cb58c2b9af081ebb0ff)

images.description: Filename of the images to delete. All images to delete require a separate field with images[] as the key (eg. images[0]=filename1.jpg, images[1]=filename2.jpg)

youtube.description: Full Youtube links to delete. All Youtube links to delete require a separate field with youtube[] as the key (eg. youtube[0]=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IGVZOLV9SPo, youtube[1]=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5nY6fjZ3EUc)

sketchfab.description: Full Sketchfab links to delete. All Sketchfab links to delete require a separate field with sketchfab[] as the key (eg. sketchfab[0]=https://sketchfab.com/models/71f04e390ff54e5f8d9a51b4e1caab7e, sketchfab[1]=https://sketchfab.com/models/5c85e649dd854cb58c2b9af081ebb0ff)

all.description: Set to true to unsubscribe from all mods.

items.description: IDs of the games to unsubscribe from.
IDs of the mods to unsubscribe from.

modfile_id.description: The id of the source modfile.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of the cook update.

metrics.description: The metrics data of the cook.

level.description:
The log severity level. May be one of: 0 (Trace), 1 (Debug), 2 (Information),
                             3 (Warning), 4 (Error), 5 (Critical), 6 (None).

is_private.description: If the log is private and should only be viewed by game moderators.

virtual_machine_type.description: A unique identifier to differentiate the virtual machine used.

display_amount.description: The amount/price that was displayed to the user. Only required for virtual token checkouts.

idempotent_key.description: The use of the idempotency key is for if in the event of a connection error, you can confidently reattempt the request without the concern of inadvertently creating a duplicate purchase or executing the purchase twice. All idempotent keys last 24 hours.

Contents of the comment. You can include @mentions to users, which will notify them that they have been tagged in this comment.

__Mention Markup__
- Format: `@`
content.example: Hey @XanT, you should check out this mod!

karma.description: Either 1 or -1 for positive and negative increments

dependencies.description: The dependencies to add to the mod. When `sync` is true and this attribute is ommitted, all dependencies will be removed.

sync.description: If true, will remove all existing dependencies and replace with the new ones provided in the request (if any).

filedata.description:
Required if the upload_id parameter is omitted. The binary file for the release. ZIP the base folder of your mod, or if it is a collection of files which live in a pre-existing game folder, you should ZIP those files. Your file must meet the following conditions:

- File must be __zipped__ and cannot exceed __500MB__ in filesize (see [Multipart Uploads](/restapi/docs/create-multipart-upload-session) to upload larger files)
- Filename's cannot contain any of the following charcters: --parse_bad_filename_chars
- Mods which span multiple game directories are not supported unless the game manages this
- Mods which overwrite files are not supported unless the game manages this

upload_id.description: Required if the filedata parameter is omitted. The UUID of a completed [multipart upload session](/restapi/docs/complete-multipart-upload-session).

version.description: Version of the file release (recommended format 1.0.0 - MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH).

active.description:
_Default value is true._ Flag this upload as the current release, this will change the `modfile` field on the parent mod to the `id` of this file after upload.

__NOTE:__ If the _active_ parameter is _true_, a [__MODFILE_CHANGED__ event](/restapi/docs/get-mod-events) will be fired, so game clients know there is an update available for this mod.

filehash.description: MD5 of the submitted file. When supplied the MD5 will be compared against the uploaded files MD5. If they don't match a `422 Unprocessible Entity` error will be returned.

metadata_blob.description: Metadata that is designed to be handled by the game client and is recommended to not be exposed to content creators when submitting their modfiles. As an example, this may include properties such as what version of the game this file is compatible with.

platforms.description: An array containing one or more [platform strings](/restapi/platforms#targeting-a-platform) that the specified modfile is targeting.

name_id.description: The name ID is used in the URL to identify the mod. It must be unique and contain only lowercase letters, numbers, and dashes.

metadata_kvp.description:
Key value pairs you want to add where the key and value are separated by a colon ':'. Every pair to add requires a separate field with metadata[] as the key (eg. metadata[]=pistol-dmg:800, metadata[]=gravity:9.8).

__NOTE:__ If the string contains multiple colons the split will occur on the first matched, eg. sword-speed-power:100:10 will become key: `sword-speed-power`, value: `100:10`). The following restrictions apply to the supplied metadata:

- Keys support alphanumeric, '_' and '-' characters only.
- Keys can map to multiple values (1-to-many relationship).
- Keys and values cannot exceed 255 characters in length.
- Key value pairs are searchable by exact match only.

metadata_blob.description: Metadata that accepts any string-based arbitrary value, up to a maximum of 50,000 characters.

appdata.description:
The Steam users [Encrypted App Ticket](https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/features/auth#encryptedapptickets) provided by the Steamworks SDK.

Parameter content *MUST* be the [*uint8 *rgubTicketEncrypted*](https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/api/SteamEncryptedAppTicket) returned after calling [ISteamUser::GetEncryptedAppTicket()](https://partner.steamgames.com/doc/api/ISteamUser#GetEncryptedAppTicket) within the Steamworks SDK, converted into a base64-encoded string.

__NOTE:__ Due to a base64-encoded string containing special characters, you must URL encode the string after it has been base64-encoded to ensure it is successfully sent to our servers otherwise you may encounter an `422 Unprocessable Entity` response. For example, [cURL](https://ec.haxx.se/http-post.html) will do this for you by using the `--data-urlencode` option.

Parameter content *MUST* be the encrypted string returned in the buffer after calling [IUser::GetEncryptedAppTicket()](https://cdn.gog.com/open/galaxy/sdk/1.133.3/Documentation/classgalaxy_1_1api_1_1IUser.html#a96af6792efc260e75daebedca2cf74c6) within the Galaxy SDK. __NOTE:__ If this is set to `false` and the user has not agreed to the latest mod.io Terms of Use and Privacy Policy, an error `403 Forbidden (error_ref 11074)` will be returned and you will need to collect the [users agreement](/restapi/docs/terms) and retry with this value set to `true` to authenticate the user. email.description:
The users email address (optional but recommended to help users recover lost accounts). If supplied, and the respective user does not have an email registered for their account we will send a confirmation email to confirm they have ownership of the specified email.

__NOTE:__ If the user already has an email on record with us, this parameter will be ignored. This parameter should also be urlencoded before the request is sent.

date_expires.description: Unix timestamp of date in which the returned token will expire. Value cannot be higher than the default value which is a week (unix timestamp + 604800 seconds). Using a token after it's expiry time has elapsed will result in a `401 Unauthorized` response.

xbox_token.description:
The Xbox Live token returned from calling [GetTokenAndSignatureAsync("POST", "https://api.mod.io")](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/microsoft.xbox.services.system.xboxliveuser.gettokenandsignatureasync?view=xboxlive-dotnet-2017.11.20171204.01).

__NOTE:__ Due to the encrypted app ticket containing special characters, you must URL encode the string before sending the request to ensure it is successfully sent to our servers otherwise you may encounter an `422 Unprocessable Entity` response. For example, [cURL](https://ec.haxx.se/http-post.html) will do this for you by using the `--data-urlencode` option.

metadata.description:
Key value pairs you want to add where the the key and value are separated by a colon ':'. Every pair to add requires a separate field with metadata[] as the key (eg. metadata[]=pistol-dmg:800, metadata[]=gravity:9.8).

__NOTE:__ If the string contains multiple colons the split will occur on the first matched, eg. sword-speed-power:100:10 will become key: `sword-speed-power`, value: `100:10`). The following restrictions apply to the supplied metadata:

- Keys support alphanumeric, '_' and '-' characters only.
- Keys can map to multiple values (1-to-many relationship).
- Keys and values cannot exceed 255 characters in length.
- Key value pairs are searchable by exact match only.

Key value pairs you want to delete where the the key and value are separated by a colon ':'. Every pair to delete requires a separate field with metadata[] as the key (eg. metadata[]=pistol-dmg:800, metadata[]=gravity:9.8).

__NOTE:__ If the string contains only the key and no colon ':', _all_ metadata with that key will be removed.

timeframe.description:
The timeframe for the moderation rules history. May be one of:

__last_minute__ = The last 60 seconds of activity.
__last_5_minutes__ = The last 5 minutes of activity.
__last_15_minutes__ = The last 15 minutes of activity.
__last_30_minutes__ = The last 30 minutes of activity.
__last_hour__ = The last 60 minutes of activity.
__last_day__ = The last 24 hours of activity.

url.description: The URL of the webhook test request. It must be a valid URL starting with 'https://'.

filename.description: The filename of the file once all the parts have been [uploaded](/restapi/docs/add-multipart-upload-part). The filename must include the `.zip` extension and cannot exceed 100 characters.

nonce.description: An optional nonce to provide to prevent duplicate upload sessions from being created concurrently. Max 64 characters (Recommended: SHA-256)
